fault_hypothesis: Line 67 uses strict greater-than (>) instead of greater-than-or-equal (>=), so a batch whose size exactly equals max_jobs is incorrectly admitted instead of rejected.

constraints.intent: When batch_size equals max_jobs the condition must also trigger rejection; strict > misses the equality case.
If batch_size equals max_jobs it must satisfy the >= condition used for rejection.
Placeholder: when batch equals max_jobs the guard on L67 with > will fail to reject — the real check needs >=.
After the rejection branch is skipped, the batch size must be strictly less than max_jobs; equality should have caused rejection.
